evals.prompt: I think we're ready to cut the v0.3.0 release. The skills-forge repo is at /sessions/zen-funny-mccarthy/mnt/skills-forge and the skill-registry is at /sessions/zen-funny-mccarthy/mnt/skill-registry. Run the full release preflight and tell me if we're good to go.
evals.expected_output: Runs all 5 phases (pytest, ruff, mypy, pack consistency, schema alignment, CLI UAT, hygiene), produces a summary table with per-phase status, and gives a clear overall verdict (green/yellow/red).

evals.prompt: Before I tag this as 1.0.0, can you verify that all the packs in the registry at /sessions/zen-funny-mccarthy/mnt/skill-registry are consistent — sha256, sizes, manifest versions all matching? Also check that the schema hasn't drifted in the skills-forge code at /sessions/zen-funny-mccarthy/mnt/skills-forge.
evals.expected_output: Runs Phase 2 (pack consistency) and Phase 3 (schema alignment), ideally also runs the other phases given it's a pre-release check. Reports per-pack pass/fail and schema alignment result.

evals.prompt: Run UAT for skills-forge at /sessions/zen-funny-mccarthy/mnt/skills-forge — I want to make sure every CLI command still works correctly before we publish to PyPI.
evals.expected_output: Runs Phase 4 CLI sandbox UAT using the uat_sandbox.py script, reports pass/fail for each of the 12 commands, gives a summary count.
